page: Microsoft Azure E96s_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 672.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage capabilities.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with high marks in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, stress-ng multi-core tests, and Geekbench multi-core execution. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top tier for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read and write operations. Cryptographic and database operations also perform strongly, as evidenced by high Passmark scores and Redis SET throughput. However, single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation represent relative bottlenecks, falling into average and weak performance tiers respectively.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU density and lack of local storage, the E96s_v5 is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring large memory footprints without local disk dependencies. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, memory-intensive enterprise applications, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. It is less optimal for GPU-dependent tasks or workloads requiring high single-threaded performance.
description: The Microsoft Azure E96s_v5 is a memory-optimized, x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. Equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM and utilizing Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads but lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmark results show strong multi-core processing, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high throughput for database operations and web serving. Conversely, single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation are average to weak. This hardware profile makes the E96s_v5 highly suitable for memory-heavy enterprise applications, relational databases, and multi-threaded software compilation where high memory density is required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v5 is a 96-vCPU memory-optimized server with 672 GB RAM, ideal for database operations and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual server featuring ninety-six virtual cores and exceptional memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 672 GB of RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput memory bandwidth
Optimized for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
No local storage or GPU resources included
Premium Storage capable for flexible remote disk configurations
